segments.text: Then Job answered,
How long will you torment me, and crush me with words?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You have reproached me ten times. You aren’t ashamed that you attack me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If it is true that I have erred, my error remains with myself.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If indeed you will magnify yourselves against me, and plead against me my reproach,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: know now that God has subverted me, and has surrounded me with his net.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard. I cry for help, but there is no justice.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He has walled up my way so that I can’t pass, and has set darkness in my paths.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He has stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He has broken me down on every side, and I am gone. He has plucked my hope up like a tree.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He has also kindled his wrath against me. He counts me among his adversaries.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
His troops come on together, build a siege ramp against me, 
and encamp around my tent.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “He has put my brothers far from me. My acquaintances are wholly estranged from me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My relatives have gone away. My familiar friends have forgotten me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Those who dwell in my house and my maids consider me a stranger. I am an alien in their sight.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I call to my servant, and he gives me no answer. I beg him with my mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My breath is offensive to my wife. I am loathsome to the children of my own mother.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even young children despise me. If I arise, they speak against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: All my familiar friends abhor me. They whom I loved have turned against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My bones stick to my skin and to my flesh. I have escaped by the skin of my teeth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “Have pity on me. Have pity on me, you my friends, for the hand of God has touched me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do you persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “Oh that my words were now written! Oh that they were inscribed in a book!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: That with an iron pen and leadthey were engraved in the rock forever!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But as for me, I know that my Redeemer lives. In the end, he will st and upon the earth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: After my skin is destroyed, then I will see God in my flesh,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
whom I, even I, will see on my side. My eyes will see, and not as a stranger. 
“My heart is consumed within me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If you say, ‘How we will persecute him!’because the root of the matter is found in me,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
be afraid of the sword, for wrath brings the punishments of the sword, 
that you may know there is a judgment.
segments.speaker: Job
